text:
RESTAURANT BRANDS ASIA COMPLETES ₹1,500 CR FUNDRAISE 🍔💰

• 📋 Shares and shares arising from warrant conversion will be listed on BSE & NSE

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens balance sheet, provides growth capital for expansion and enhances financial flexibility)

text:
JEWELLERY STOCKS ; GOVT TIGHTENS SILVER IMPORT RULES

Govt mandates DGFT nod for silver imports through banks, nominated agencies and qualified jewellers

Silver imports through India International Bullion Exchange (IIBX) also brought under regime

Move marks latest step by govt to tighten oversight of precious metal imports

Move follows recent hike in gold and silver import duties to 15% amid concerns over rising import bill, pressure on forex reserves

India imports most of its silver requirements from UAE, UK and China

Silver is used across jewellery manufacturing as well as industrial applications

text:
DRDO & IAF SUCCESSFULLY TEST RUDRAM-II MISSILE 🚀🇮🇳

• 🎯 DRDO and IAF successfully conducted flight tests of the indigenous RudraM-II Air-to-Surface Missile

• ✈️ Missile launched from an airborne platform under extreme release conditions

• 🎯 Achieved pin-point accuracy, successfully hitting the designated target

• ✅ All mission objectives met; performance validated through flight data from ITR Chandipur

• 🏭 Developed by DRDO's Research Centre Imarat (RCI) with support from multiple DRDO labs and industry partners

• 🛩️ HAL, certification agencies and defence industry partners contributed to the program

• 🇮🇳 Defence Minister Rajnath Singh hailed the success as a major boost to Aatmanirbhar Bharat in advanced weapon systems

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens India's indigenous missile capabilities and benefits the domestic defence ecosystem, including DRDO, HAL and defence manufacturing partners)

Wockhardt Targets Zaynich's $1.5 Billion US Sales Prescription; Peak Annual Sales Potential $1.5-2 Billion Against $9 Billion Global Market — 15-20% Market Share Targeted, India Launch in Months, Europe by Late 2026

The Commercial Launch Plan — Timeline & Markets
Wockhardt targeting commercial launch of Zaynich in the US by end of calendar year 2026 or early 2027

India expected to follow within a few months — DCGI approval already received though commercialisation requires completion of local clearances related to manufacturing

Wockhardt expects the product to be launched sometime towards the end of this year, maybe early next year — Habil Khorakiwala, Founder & Chairman

European regulatory decisions expected from European Medicines Agency (EMA) and UK's MHRA later this year — approvals expected around September-October 2026

Commercial launches in Europe could take another year as country-specific pricing and reimbursement negotiations must be completed ahead of entry

The Market Opportunity — Size & Target
Peak annual sales potential of $1.5-2 billion for Zaynich globally

Estimated $9 billion global market opportunity for resistant Gram-negative infections

Wockhardt believes it can get 15-20% market share over a period of time

Only 5 antibiotics have completed Phase III trials in this space — limited competition

Zaynich designed to combat multidrug-resistant Gram-negative pathogens including E. coli, Klebsiella pneumoniae, Enterobacter cloacae and Pseudomonas aeruginosa

In its pivotal Phase-III Enhance-1 study, demonstrated composite clinical cure and microbiological response rate of 89% — compared with 68.4% for Meropenem

Pricing Strategy — Price Neutral Approach
New-generation antibiotics in US currently cost $10,000-15,000 per treatment course — Wockhardt intends to remain broadly within that range

"Our approach strategically is we want to be price neutral. Even though the product is far superior, we are taking price neutrality as an approach" — Khorakiwala

India price would be a fraction of the US cost — perhaps 15-20% of that level

Addressable patient pool in India estimated at around 2,50,000 patients

The Go-To-Market Model — Lean Organisation
Wockhardt plans to commercialise Zaynich on its own in the US — building a lean organisation focused on medical affairs, sales, marketing and market access

Outsourcing field-force and operational activities

Will supply Zaynich to the US and other global markets via third-party manufacturing facilities in Europe — primarily in Italy — while Indian demand will be met through local plants

Manufacturing from Europe could also shield the product from any future US tariffs on Indian pharmaceutical imports

Has transferred its active pharma ingredient and formulation technology to European partners whose facilities were reviewed by FDA as part of the approval process

Wockhardt Stock — Market Reaction
Stock jumped 21.5% since the FDA approval announcement — closing at ₹2,151.85 on the BSE

First new chemical entity fully discovered and developed by an Indian pharmaceutical company to receive USFDA approval

Core Theme 
Wockhardt's Zaynich commercialisation strategy is a masterclass in risk-calibrated global pharma expansion — by adopting price neutrality to maximise market access, building a lean US commercial organisation, manufacturing in Europe to hedge tariff risks, and targeting a $9 billion antimicrobial resistance market with a drug that demonstrated 89% efficacy versus 68.4% for the standard of care, the company is positioning India's first fully indigenous novel antibiotic for peak sales of $1.5-2 billion; the next 12-18 months will determine whether Wockhardt can convert this landmark scientific achievement into a transformative commercial success that redefines India's standing in global pharmaceutical innovation.

RELIANCE GETS MAJOR SUPREME COURT RELIEF IN 2007 RPL CASE ⚖️🏛️

• ✅ Supreme Court ruled that 'fraud' under PFUTP Regulations is not made out against Reliance Industries (RIL)

• 🔄 Set aside SAT and SEBI findings that had held RIL guilty of fraudulent trading in Reliance Petroleum (RPL) shares

• ❌ Quashed the disgorgement order of ₹447.27 Crore plus interest imposed on RIL

• 📜 Court held that SAT committed an error in confirming the fraud allegations under PFUTP Regulations

• ⚠️ However, Supreme Court upheld that RIL had violated disclosure requirements under the 2001 SEBI and NSE circulars relating to position limits

• 💰 Penalty linked to disclosure lapses remains valid

• 📅 Judgment passed on May 29, 2026, in appeals arising from the long-running 2007 RPL derivatives matter

🟢 Impact: Positive (Removal of the major fraud finding and ₹447+ crore disgorgement liability is a significant legal victory for Reliance, though disclosure-related penalties continue to stand.)

*Nomura on Data Centre*
A decadal growth opportunity
India’s datacenter (DC) industry to record +30% CAGR over CY25-30F & outperform broader APAC
Lower capex and favourable power economics support attractive DC returns
India’s digital, cloud and GenAI adoption driving DC demand
Robust demand and sustainable premium pricing trends unlock attractive DC opportunities for industrial equipment manufacturers
Estimate five product categories
(i) medium- and low-voltage switchgear and transformers,
(ii) UPS and battery systems,
(iii) backup diesel and gas gensets,
(iv) precision cooling and liquid-cooling distribution units, and
(v) rack, busway, and structured cabling infrastructure together absorb 60-75% of a DC's USD10-22mn/MW capex budget
Competitive landscape in India is consolidated and pricing disciplined with players such as ABB India Reduce), Siemens (Neutral), Hitachi Energy (Not rated), GE Vernova (Buy), CG Power (Buy), Cummins (Neutral), etc., acting as oligopolistic suppliers across multiple sub-categories

*Morgan Stanley On India Autos*
Preferred picks are Maruti Suzuki, Mahindra, TVS and Hero
May 2026 retail sales healthy across passenger vehicles, two-wheelers, light commercial vehicles and tractors
Passenger vehicle and two-wheeler retail sales grew 27% and 11% year on year respectively; tractor retail up 16% year on year
Medium and heavy commercial vehicle growth moderated to 6% year on year; fleet operators under pressure from higher input costs and diesel prices
Electric vehicle penetration improving; two-wheeler electric share at 9.2% in May vs 7.9% in April; passenger vehicle electric share at 6.4% vs 5.9% in April
Mahindra flagged supply chain constraints from manpower shortages at select suppliers; could have added 10% growth in utility vehicles and light commercial vehicles without these
Isolated supply headwinds, commodity cost increases and price hikes are key monitorables going forward
channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

Healthy growth in 2W/PV while slow in CVs

PV and CV retail volumes were sustained on the back of resilient demand

*CLSA on Zee Ent*
O-P, TP Rs 125
Zee Entertainment has won FIFA’s 39 global football events rights to broadcast & stream in India which include FIFA World cup 2026 and 2030
Zee will showcase FIFA events for eight consecutive years across Unite8 Sports channels & OTT/streaming service Zee5
While cricket is predominant sports viewership in India, FIFA is global sport & this win will enable Zee strengthen presence in sports advertising and subscriptions.
Also, Zee has likely won FIFA rights at reported valuation of under US$60m which is lower than Rs4.5bn paid by Viacom 18/Reliance media co for FIFA World cup 2022.
India CY25 sports revenues were Rs223bn/US$2.4bn of which cricket accounted for 90% share.
Furthermore, with Zee5 breaking even, Zee’s strong balance sheet (Rs27.6bn cash) & stock valuation attractive at under 1x sales and 12x PE for FY27CL, retain O-P

*Axis Capital On Zee*
Recommendation Reduce, Target ₹80
Zee partners with FIFA for broadcasting rights in India
Accretion to ad and subscription rev could be limited by the match timings
ZEE5 could see a boost in subscription revenues in the season
ZEE5 it is unlikely to be sticky post the flagship events
Current estimates assume 4% subscription CAGR for FY26-29E

*HSBC on Glenmark*
Buy, TP Rs 2575
4QFY26 revenue in line led by the Europe segment, which offset lower than estimated sales in US and India
US sales pick-up key, driven by gFloVent and other respiratory products and supplies from Monroe plant
Guidance of INR17-18bn revenue & 21-22% EBITDA margin in FY27

*HSBC on UPL*
Buy, TP Rs 880
Management meet takeaways
Management continues to believe in valueunlocking potential through the reorganisation announced in February.
Per UPL, this structure allows the group synergies to be best harnessed compared to other options.
HSBC view – While reorganization requires approvals at several levels, including from minority shareholders, if this goes ahead, it would subject UPL Ltd. (listed entity) to a holding company discount partly offsetting any value unlocking
UPL currently trades at an EV/EBITDA of 6x FY28e

text:
*Jefferies on Guj Gas*
Upgrade to Hold, TP Rs 415
GUJGA delivered a beat on volume and margins.
It is benefiting from nonavailability of propane in Morbi, leading to market share gain and margin expansion
Introduce gas trading business in model with amalgamation effective
Have raised CGD Ebitda by 45% for FY27E on the back of strong Morbi volumes.

*Jefferies on Concord Bio*
Hold, TP Rs 1020
Concord Mar'26-Q numbers were weak & missed estimates on delayed customer orders, approval delays and change in customer procurement pattern.
For FY27, mgmt. guided for higher revenue growth vs historical avg (imply it as ~20% growth) with EBITDA margin improvement.
Concord has visibility for 1HFY27 orderbook but some of challenges like customer tender delays are not fully behind.

*Kotak Inst Eqt on CONCORD Bio*
ADD, TP Rs 1275
Reported a sharp miss in 4QFY26
While muted FY2026 was driven by extrinsic issues (tariff uncertainty, airspace constraints, tender delays and staggered procurement patterns), oversupply in the small molecule fermentation API space remains a headache.
Concord is confident about a recovery hereon, backed by high 1HFY27 visibility and is guiding for 18%+ yoy sales growth in FY2027E.
Backed by strength of its franchise, continue to factor in a pick-up in sales hereon (+24%/21% yoy in FY2027/28E).
On a low base (30% EPS fall in FY2026), expect Concord to report ~29% EPS CAGR over FY2026-29E, led by higher utilization and new launches, aided by market share gains in existing molecules.

*Jefferies on Finolex Cables*
Buy, TP Raised to Rs 1200 from Rs1000
FNXC is a key C&W domestic players & a play on housing, capex.
Despite +30% rise YTD, stock trades at 21x/18x FY27/28e PE, steeply below peers & in-line with its hist 10Y avg PE of 19x.
Mar26-Q EBITDA was a beat.
Electricals (~85% of sales) posted healthy volume in Cables (Auto, Flexible, Power).
FNXC took many price hikes in FY26 (total ~20%) to pass-on copper volatility
OFC is a good opportunity & FMEG sustains +ive EBIT.

*CITI on NMDC*
Sell, TP Rs 85
At ~Rs26.4bn (2% below), 4Q EBITDA rose ~29% yoy - higher volumes (+21%), likely lower costs (excluding royalty and steel/pellet purchases), partly offset by lower realizations (-3%)
EBITDA margin: 24% vs. 29% in 3Q and last year (margin lower due to steel trading).
Call takeaways:
(1) Mgmt expects prices to be rangebound,
(2) FY26 production target ~60mt, vs. ~53mt in FY25, in their view, there is enough demand
(3) FY26 capex was Rs33bn, NMDC expects ~Rs60bn in FY27; Rs70-100bn pa thereafter (target: 100mt end of the decade).
(4) NMDC engages in HRC trading if there is a cash flow issue for NMDC Steel (need basis).
Sell - expect domestic prices to be rangebound: (1) Global price outlook, (2) NMDC’s volume focus, (3) Lloyds Metal and Energy has enhanced ore capacity from 10mt to 25mt.

*Systematix On NMDC*
Maintain BUY on NMDC with a revised target price of Rs 112
Strong FY26 execution with future growth trajectory intact
Management has guided for 60mt production in FY27
Management expects EBITDA margin to recover to ~35-40%
Raises FY27E/FY28E EBITDA estimates by 10%/8% and PAT estimates by 23%/21%
Revision is built on NMDC’s strong execution track record

*Investec On Natco Pharma*
Recommendation Buy, Target ₹1220, Earlier Target ₹1035
Q4 saw Core biz performing well – after adjusting for one-off expense
No gRevlimid but delivered strong numbers
Sema-India (~1bn sales) and new launches in Brazil/Canada/US as key drivers
With Rs 2500cr cash, co offers M&A optionality – management has been conservative
Raise FY27E/FY28E EPS ests by 5%/4%

*Investec On Cello World*
Recommendation Sell; Target ₹435, Earlier Target ₹515
Expect downgrades to continue
Opalware continues to see increase in competitive intensity
Glassware faces constant pressure from imports further delaying ramp-up
Receivables rationalization to impact growth and margins
Reduce target multiple to 24x (30x, DCF derived) and PAT ests. are 26/13% lower

text:
TRUMP EASES TARIFFS ON CERTAIN STEEL & ALUMINIUM-BASED EQUIPMENT 🇺🇸🏗️

• 🏭 US President Donald Trump signed a proclamation updating tariffs on steel, aluminium and copper-related imports

• 📉 Tariffs on certain agricultural equipment (combines, harvesters, etc.) reduced from 25% to 15%

• 🚜 15% tariff category expanded to include mobile industrial equipment such as bulldozers and forklifts

• 🇺🇸 Foreign manufacturers can qualify for a lower 10% tariff if equipment contains at least 85% US-produced steel or aluminium

• ⏳ Measures are temporary and will remain effective until 31 December 2027

• 💰 Objective is to boost domestic manufacturing, encourage investment and increase use of US-made metals

Likely Impact on Indian Companies

• 🟢 Exporters of engineering goods, capital equipment and auto components may benefit from lower tariffs on select equipment categories

• 🟢 Positive for Indian manufacturers supplying equipment used in agriculture, construction and industrial sectors

• 🟡 Impact on steel and aluminium exporters remains mixed as broader US metal trade policies continue to favor domestic production

🟢 Impact: Positive for Select Indian Engineering & Capital Goods Exporters (Lower tariffs on certain equipment categories could improve competitiveness and export opportunities in the US market.)

BIG MOVE by MCX

text:
Sacheerome H2 FY26 Earnings Call Highlights

👉 FY27 & Future Outlook:
▫️ Revenue guidance: Conservative estimate of ₹200+ Cr in FY27, eventually crossing ₹300+ Cr in FY29. 
💠Management noted the company’s pipeline, customer demand, and capacity expansion to support these estimates.
💠 Operating margins expected to remain stable around 25% in FY27 despite higher depreciation from the new facility and ongoing geopolitical challenges
💠 Long-term focus on sustainable, profitable growth with emphasis on innovation, product customization, and scaling both domestic and international presence.

👉 Current Projects and Future Pipeline:
▫️ Existing operations running at ~120-124% capacity utilization in FY26, driving strong revenue growth despite constraints. 
💠New YEIDA (Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Area) facility will multiply production capacity ~4x
💠Expected to commence commercial operations in August 2026 (with some possibility of July start).
💠 Total capex for the new facility ~₹184 Cr; ₹76.59 Cr already deployed as of 31 March 2026 (₹28.79 Cr from IPO + ₹47.8 Cr from internal accruals). 
💠Remaining spend will be phased, with ~₹60 Cr planned via debt as and when required; company currently almost debt-free with good surplus.
💠 The new facility is a strategic investment focused on enhanced automation, efficiency, product consistency, export capabilities, and scalability. 
💠It will support both fragrance (currently ~94% of revenue) and flavor (~6%) segments, addressing unmet demand from existing and new customers.
💠Management highlighted significant headroom as the company is still very small relative to the overall fragrance & flavor market opportunity.
💠 Customer pipeline remains healthy with demand from domestic FMCG players (94% of revenue) and growing exports (6% of revenue, up in absolute value).
💠Growth in FY27 will be driven by continued over-utilization of existing capacity in H1 and contribution from the new facility in H2. 
💠Additional land available for further expansion in coming years depending on demand.

👉 Other Notable Points:
▫️ FY26 performance: 
💠Revenue split: new customers contributed ~₹13.61 Cr, existing customers ~₹31.24 Cr. Domestic 94%, exports 6%; fragrance 94%, flavors 6%.
💠 Margin improvement driven by operating leverage — turnover grew significantly while operating costs rose only ~4%. 
💠R&D spend currently ~2.3% of sales; business is innovation-driven with focus on customer-specific briefs and proprietary technologies embedded in products.
💠 Company is debt-free with a improving working capital cycle. 
💠Participating in World Perfumery Congress (June 2026, California) to boost international visibility and relationships.
💠 Key priorities: deepening existing customer ties, expanding to new segments/geographies, continued innovation, operational efficiency, and disciplined international expansion.

HPCL - Bullish Note

Morgan Stanley on HPCL – Tactical Buy; 50% upside

Maintain Overweight with TP of Rs 610 

Believe the share price will rise in absolute terms over the next 30 days

Levered play on energy security and improving supply of crude oil

Net realization for fuel has seen its loss run rate reduce to nearly US$7-9/bbl outside of cooking gas

See potential reduction in crude premiums and improved EBIDTA quality as key triggers in coming weeks

@sTOCKSIP

Goldman Sachs on PhysicsWallah

Maintain Neutral

Target price raised to Rs 133 from Rs 130

Q4 revenue growth accelerated to 51% YoY, beating estimates

Elara Capital UPGRADES Sudarshan Chemical:

Upgrade to Buy from Accumulate
Target price raised to Rs 1,212 from Rs 1,047

Q4 recovery driven by acquired Heubach business.

Elara Capital UPGRADES Sudarshan Chemical:

Upgrade to Buy from Accumulate
Target price raised to Rs 1,212 from Rs 1,047

Q4 recovery driven by acquired Heubach business

BOFA ON INDIA CAPEX THEMES 🏗📈

• BofA says India's Shipbuilding sector is where EMS industry was around 7 years ago, indicating a potential long-term growth opportunity

• Brokerage views Shipbuilding as a new frontier of manufacturing-led growth

• Continues to like Data Centres, Shipbuilding & Transformer sectors

• However, believes transformer stocks now appear quite expensive after the sharp run-up

• Remains cautious on sectors heavily dependent on state government capex spending

Motilal Oswal on ONGC: 

Maintain Neutral Rating with target price of ₹265

Siemens In Focus – Negative – Two Downgrades

Kotak Securities on Siemens
Downgrade to Sell from Reduce; TP at Rs 2950

Not as much localized; getting hit, finding it difficult to alter
Results bring out the impact of limited localization, which is difficult to alter
Analyst call brings out aspect of timing issues in weakness in results
Guarded outlook on top of 8-10% ordering growth seen in recent past

Jefferies on Siemens
Downgrade to Hold from Buy; TP at Rs 4000

Subdued Margin Outlook
Lower FY26E-27 EPS by 23-34% to factor margin pressure
Believe there could be scope for downgrades if prices hikes are insufficient

Cantor on Adani Ports

Maintain Overweight; Hike TP to Rs 2178 from Rs 1719

Ambition 2031 Reinforces Integrated Transport Thesis
FY27 guidance is constructive yet intentionally conservative
Post-earnings message is that APSEZ remains a compounding infrastructure platform
Domestic ports remain the earnings anchor
International ports were a key upside driver
Logistics and marine continue to scale as strategic growth pillars
Ambition 2031 is the most important incremental disclosure
Capital allocation remains disciplined
Morgan Stanley on Ashok Leyland: Maintain Equal-weight; target price at ₹180
BofA initiates 'Buy' ratings on Lenskart with target price of Rs 575.

Jefferies downgraded Siemens India to Hold

Sees only 3% upside from current levels

**PINE LABS – JEFFERIES REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price cut to ₹185 from ₹260

**Q4 Performance**
- Core earnings missed estimates
- Revenue grew 17% YoY
- Contribution margin stood at 73%

**Profitability**
- Adjusted EBITDA margin expanded to 21%
- Lower operating expense growth supported margins
- One-time gains helped profit reach ₹585 Mn

**Cash Flow**
- Improved cash flow generation was a key positive

**Outlook**
- Management expects revenue growth to accelerate from Q1
- Growth expected to reach 20%+
- FY27 revenue growth guided at 21–23%
- Driven by higher POS deployment
- Supported by growth in issuing and affordability revenues

**FINOLEX INDUSTRIES – JEFFERIES REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹220

**Valuation**
- Stock up 11% in the last 5 days
- Trades at 19x FY27E earnings
- Valuation in line with 10-year historical average

**Business Mix**
- Agri segment contributes 65% of pipes mix
- Viewed as a play on agriculture and rural demand
- Plumbing segment now contributes 37%
- Targeting ~50% plumbing mix over next 5 years

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- PAT exceeded Jefferies estimates
- Operating margin improved to 25%
- Compared with 14.6% in the year-ago period

**Volume Trends**
- Q4 volume remained flat YoY
- FY26 volume declined 4%
- Unseasonal rainfall impacted demand

**Earnings Outlook**
- FY27–FY28 EPS estimates raised by 3–8%

**GROWW – JEFFERIES REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹225

**Management Meet Takeaways**
- Wealth management strategy
- Share gain opportunities in core broking
- MTF scale-up
- Customer behavior in consumer lending
- Regulatory developments

**Key Debate**
- Bulls appreciate execution capabilities
- Cross-sell opportunities remain attractive
- Bears question scalability of wealth management
- Regulatory risks remain a concern

**Valuation**
- Trading at PEG of 1.1x
- Risk-reward seen as favorable

**MPHASIS – BROKERAGE REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**SBI CAPITAL MARKETS (SBC)**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹2,780
- Launched Tria platform to transition from services-led model to platform-plus-outcomes model
- Performance to be measured through Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR)
- Strong AI demand lifted TTM TCV above USD 2 Bn
- Supports high single- to double-digit revenue growth over FY26–FY28E
- Valuations remain reasonable
- Revenue growth trajectory has improved

**NOMURA**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹2,620
- Focusing on an “agency model” to compete in the AI era
- Large deal teams helping win more deals
- Delivery model being redesigned for AI-led execution at scale
- Maintaining disciplined capital allocation
- Continuing to invest for future growth
- Pivoting towards an outcome-based business model

**JPMORGAN**
- Overweight rating maintained
- Target price: ₹2,650

**Key Takeaways from Analyst Day**
- AI implementation in brownfield projects remains complex and requires deep enterprise context
- Launched AI platform Tria with subscription, implementation and managed services pricing
- Strategic engagement team helping expand small transformation projects into larger multi-year deals
- Following a balance-sheet intensive strategy of buying out contracts from tail vendors
- Transitioning from a services-led model to a platform-plus-outcome model from FY27
- Plans to report new metrics from FY28 including ARR, platform attach rate and net retention rate

**CITI**
- Neutral rating maintained
- Target price: ₹2,310
- Management introduced Mphasis Tria as the core strategy for accelerating enterprise agency
- Focused on helping clients convert AI capabilities into governed and economic outcomes
- Leveraging platform-led offerings to capture emerging enterprise AI demand
- Remains cautious on the IT services sector
- Continues to monitor AI's impact on long-term sector growth and valuations

**GMR AIRPORTS – BROKERAGE REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**JEFFERIES**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹125
- Q4 EBITDA at ₹14.8 Bn vs estimate of ₹15.3 Bn
- EBITDA grew 47% YoY but declined 13% QoQ
- FY26 EBITDA rose 60% YoY to ₹60 Bn
- Growth achieved despite only 1% YoY passenger growth
- Q4 impacted by weaker international traffic
- Higher expenses at Hyderabad Airport weighed on profitability
- GAL platform EBITDA nearly doubled YoY in FY26
- Company reported full-year positive PAT after several years
- Net debt declined QoQ

**CITI**
- MarQFY26 core profit at ₹1.3 Bn
- Compared with loss of ₹2.5 Bn in MarQFY25
- Lower than ₹3.2 Bn reported in DecQFY26
- Core profit was 18% below consensus estimates
- FY26 earnings achieved 125% of Street FY26 estimates
- Reported NPAT included one-off gains
- Included ~₹1 Bn claim from Crete Airport
- Included ₹1.2 Bn reversal of Hyderabad tax liability
- MarQFY26 revenue was 1% below Street estimates
- MarQFY26 EBITDA was 9% below Street estimates

**Traffic & Non-Aero Revenue**
- Passenger traffic at key airports declined 1% QoQ
- Passenger traffic increased 1% YoY
- International traffic declined 2% YoY
- Domestic traffic increased 2% YoY
- Combined non-aero revenue grew 6% YoY
- Average passenger spending increased 5% YoY

**Airport-wise Performance**
- Delhi profit declined 47% QoQ to ₹1.2 Bn
- Hyderabad profit increased 108% QoQ to ₹1.8 Bn
- Goa losses narrowed 15% QoQ to ₹0.6 Bn

**Balance Sheet**
- Net debt stood at ₹340 Bn in Mar-26
- Improved from ₹345 Bn in Dec-25

**SIEMENS – BROKERAGE REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**HSBC**
- Hold rating maintained
- Target price: ₹3,540
- Order inflows remained strong
- Execution performance continued to be robust
- Higher material costs weighed on earnings in the March quarter
- Capex cycle remains steady so far
- Margin recovery may be more gradual than earlier expectations
- Current valuation adequately captures medium-term earnings growth and execution optimism

**NOMURA**
- Neutral rating maintained
- Target price: ₹3,700
- Order inflow grew 33% YoY in the March 2026 quarter
- Growth led by the Mobility (MO) segment
- Significant opportunities emerging from data center capex investments
- Commodity prices likely to pressure margins
- Rupee depreciation expected to create additional margin headwinds
- FY27F EBITDA margin estimated to be 73 bps lower than TTM levels
- Stock trading at 60x FY28F EPS

GMR Airports gains brokerage support after Q4FY26 results ✈️

• Jefferies: Buy | TP ₹125

• Citi: Neutral | TP ₹105

Jefferies highlighted 60% YoY EBITDA growth in FY26, full-year positive PAT after several years, and declining net debt.

Citi noted profit recovery and improving non-aero revenues, though Q4 revenue and EBITDA came in below Street estimates.

**ASHOK LEYLAND – BROKERAGE REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**HSBC**
- Hold rating maintained
- Target price: ₹180
- Management expects recovery from Q2FY27
- HSBC believes full recovery may take longer
- Most commodity cost impact likely to flow through in Q1FY27
- Near-term demand uncertainty remains a challenge

**CITI**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹206
- Q4 slightly above estimates
- Gross margin surprised positively
- Underlying demand remains resilient despite diesel price hikes
- Volumes could rebound in H2FY27 due to pent-up demand
- Export retail demand remains steady despite logistics disruptions
- Structural CV growth drivers remain intact
- Minor FY27/FY28 EBITDA estimate cuts due to commodity inflation and softer demand outlook

**CLSA**
- Outperform rating maintained
- Target price cut to ₹183
- Q4 EBITDA margin at 14.6%, in line with consensus
- Gross margin improved 82 bps QoQ
- Supported by January price hike and value re-engineering initiatives
- Raw material costs remain inflationary in Q1
- Company implemented ~1.5% price hikes to offset cost pressures
- Remains cautiously optimistic on M&HCV demand growth
- GST rate cuts and replacement demand expected to support growth

**JPMORGAN**
- Neutral rating maintained
- Target price: ₹175
- Q4 slightly missed JPM estimates
- EBITDA at ₹20.7 Bn (+15% YoY)
- Higher staff and other expenses impacted profitability
- Management did not provide explicit FY27 growth or margin guidance
- Demand drivers remain robust
- Fuel price hikes and disruptions may cause near-term demand dislocation
- Industry pricing discipline expected to continue
- Multiple price hikes likely across the sector to offset commodity inflation

**ALKEM LABORATORIES – NOMURA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹6,890

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Revenue was in line with estimates
- EBITDA beat estimates by 11%
- Gross margin improved to 65.4%
- Gross margin expanded by 606 bps YoY

**Exceptional Items**
- Exceptional loss of ₹1.35 Bn reported
- ₹747 Mn impairment charge on real estate investments
- ₹603 Mn impact from changes in the Labor Code

**PAT Performance**
- PAT impacted by exceptional items
- Higher effective tax rate also affected profitability
- Exceptional charges are one-off in nature

**FY27 Outlook**
- FY27 guidance was in line with or ahead of current expectations

**Valuation**
- Trades at 23.6x FY27F adjusted EPS
- Trades at 19.7x FY28F adjusted EPS
- FY27F adjusted EPS estimated at ₹230.8
- FY28F adjusted EPS estimated at ₹276.2

**APAR INDUSTRIES – NOMURA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price raised to ₹14,240

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Strong Q4FY26 performance
- EBITDA beat Nomura estimates by 3%
- EBITDA beat consensus estimates by 5%

**Conductor Business**
- Conductor EBITDA/MT grew 8% YoY in Q4FY26
- Demand remains structurally healthy

**Cable Business**
- Robust growth continues in cables
- DC cables offer premium pricing opportunities
- Strong demand outlook for DC cable segment

**Capex Plans**
- FY27 capex guidance at ₹15 Bn
- Investments aimed at supporting future growth

**Earnings Outlook**
- FY27F EBITDA estimate raised by 7%
- FY28F EBITDA estimate raised by 9%
- EBITDA CAGR of 19% expected over FY26–FY29F

**Valuation**
- Trades at 37x FY27F EPS
- Trades at 29x FY28F EPS

**Investment View**
- Outlook remains on firm footing
- Strong demand across key business segments supports growth visibility

**VINATI ORGANICS – KOTAK INSTITUTIONAL EQUITIES REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Sell rating maintained
- Target price: ₹1,200

**FY26 Performance**
- Revenue declined 1% in FY26
- Decline driven by lower revenues from IBB and ATBS

**Management Guidance**
- Management continues to guide for 15% volume growth in FY27

**Business Challenges**
- VOPL projects continue to struggle
- Market conditions remain a risk
- Competitive intensity continues to be a concern

**Earnings Outlook**
- Kotak reduced EPS estimates by a further 9–10%

**CUMMINS INDIA – NOMURA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Downgraded to Neutral
- Target price: ₹6,000
- Stock considered fully priced in

**Growth Outlook**
- Demand drivers remain intact
- PAT CAGR of 18% expected over FY26–FY29F
- Domestic demand outlook remains constructive

**Q4 Performance**
- EBITDA beat estimates by 7%–9%
- Operating leverage offset gross margin contraction

**Margin Outlook**
- Near-term gross margin headwinds expected
- Commodity inflation may outpace pricing actions
- Margin pressure likely in the short term

**Valuation**
- Stock trades at 52x FY28F EPS
- Limited upside at current valuations

**ONGC – CLSA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- High Conviction Outperform
- Target price: ₹405

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- 4Q standalone PAT missed estimates by 22%
- Post-results analyst call revealed several one-off charges
- Adjusted core EBIT was in line with estimates
- Adjusted PBT was also in line with estimates

**Production Outlook**
- Management did not provide specific near-term guidance on production ramp-up
- Remains confident about new field development
- New fields expected to add more than 30% to gas production over the next two years
- Production additions expected to start from next quarter

**Mumbai High**
- Service contract with British Petroleum (BP) expected to provide support
- Likely to temporarily halt production decline at Mumbai High

**Valuation**
- Stock currently pricing in Brent crude at US$62/bbl
- CLSA sees over 44% upside using US$82/bbl Brent assumption for FY28
- Upside increases to 73% at current spot Brent of US$95/bbl

**BDL – GS REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Sell rating maintained
- Target price cut to ₹1,260

**Execution Concerns**
- Execution remains a key concern
- Company missed expected execution for the seventh consecutive year

**Reasons for FY27 Miss**
- Domestic supply chain issues
- Unavailability of a component from an overseas supplier

**Revenue Performance**
- FY26 revenue declined 27% YoY
- Underperformed compared to other defence peers under coverage

**Inventory Build-Up**
- Inventory surged 75% YoY to ₹46.3 Bn
- Inventory exceeds 2x FY26 revenue
- Increase mainly due to finished goods awaiting delivery

**Order Book Strength**
- FY26 order book estimated at ₹257 Bn
- Implies >10x book-to-bill ratio
- Highest among companies under coverage

**Order Inflow Outlook**
- FY27 order inflow expected at ₹140 Bn
- Order pipeline remains strong

**Key Monitorable**
- Pick-up in execution will be the key factor to watch

**MCX – UBS REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Downgraded to Neutral
- Target price raised to ₹3,600 from ₹3,200

**Earnings Outlook**
- Peak earnings momentum likely behind

**Volume Trends**
- Elevated volatility across key commodities in recent months
- Healthy Q1FY27-to-date volumes
- Volumes have normalized from Q4 levels

**Competition**
- Not worried about competition as of now

**Valuation**
- Stock trading at ~50x one-year forward P/E
- Around 10% above historical average valuation

**Investment View**
- Strong volume run-rate already priced in
- Limited upside seen from current levels
JPMorgan maintains 'Overweight' ratings on Mphasis with target price of Rs 2,650

Citi  maintains 'Buy' ratings on Ashok Leyland with target price of Rs 205

Morgan Stanley maintains 'Equal-weight' ratings on Ashok Leyland with target price of Rs 180.
Macquarie maintains 'Neutral' ratings on Siemens with target price of Rs 3,510

**High Upside Calls**

- Systematix sees 51% upside in Krsnaa Diagnostics.
- Systematix sees 50% upside in Orchid Pharma.
- Axis Capital sees 61% upside in Pine Labs.
- PL Capital sees 36% upside in IRCTC.

**Infrastructure & Industrial Plays**
- Motilal Oswal sees 26% upside in KEC.
- Motilal Oswal sees 23% upside in Syrma SGS.
- Motilal Oswal sees 13% upside in Astra Microwave.
- Motilal Oswal sees 31% upside in Zensar.

**Consumer & Retail**
- Anand Rathi initiated BUY on Cello World.
- Emkay initiated BUY on SJS Enterprises.
- Motilal positive on ABFRL growth momentum.
- Sharekhan downgraded ABFRL to Hold.

**Healthcare & Pharma**
- Axis positive on Jubilant Ingrevia CDMO growth.
- PL Capital cautious on Jubilant Ingrevia valuations.
- PL sees 18% upside in Narayana Hrudayalaya.
- JM sees limited upside in Artemis Medicare.

**Real Estate & Financials**
- PL sees 28% upside in Prestige Estates.
- Motilal positive on Kolte Patil revival.
- PL sees 23% upside in Kotak Mahindra Bank.

**Energy & Utilities**

**Technology & New Economy**

**Final Takeaway**
- Brokerages remain selectively bullish on manufacturing, healthcare, infrastructure, electronics and energy transition themes.
